corpus.corpus_hash_basis: sha256 tree over canonicalJson of corrections-export/v1 records (includeRetracted:true), computed pre-harvest — the §4.1 ingestion projection
corpus.scored_set: active-counted (all per_item rows; lead-in-unusable rows flagged redaction_survived:false, counted in denominators, never fired)
corpus.prior_universe: all-counted-including-retracted (harvested CTIs; a prior contributes recorded fields only, its own lead-in quality is irrelevant)
corpus.prior_join: same-project same-class (cluster-signature overlap >= MIN_OVERLAP; predict-loo's join per §2.1 — the §3.2 pseudocode is project-silent; the unscoped cross-project reading was tested and rejected: generic rule tokens collide across projects, diluting deriveBlindSpots trigger sets below MIN_OVERLAP and structurally zeroing the keyword path while inflating `predictable`)
corpus.reader_note: Denominators here are NOT comparable to predict-loo's: predict-loo raw-reads every project JSON (silently dropping rule/date fails) and scores ALL records incl. retracted as targets with per-project priors; correction-transfer ingests via exportCorrections (§4.1), scores active-counted targets only, and uses a cross-project all-counted prior universe. Different readers, different denominators — by construction, both published (§2.2).

_note: transform-not-regenerated: slug anonymization applied as a pure string transform to preserve the historical corpus snapshot (2026-07-03). corpus_hash is unchanged (hashes cover canonicalJson of correction records, not project-wrapper strings). Tool: scripts/eval/anonymize-baseline.mjs.
